_meta.note: Ground truth example. Input preserved for reproducibility.

_input.goal: Reach $10K MRR within 90 days with a two-person team
_input.outputs: 3 paying customers ($450 MRR), 12 demo calls booked last month, 2 blog posts published

summary: Inbound demo conversion and direct customer calls are the entire engine — everything else is noise at this MRR stage.

vital_20.insight: 3 customers came from inbound — this is already working. Speed-to-response is the only variable that matters here. Every hour of delay costs conversion.
Direct voice contact compresses the feedback loop that all other activities approximate. At sub-$1K MRR, founder-led discovery is the highest-ROI motion that exists.
Only the features surfaced directly from discovery calls qualify as vital. Roadmap items not traceable to a named customer are trivial.

trivial_80.prune_reason: Zero attributed customers at $450 MRR with 3 customers means cold outreach has not closed anything. At 50/day it is consuming the most calendar time for the least validated return. Pause until inbound is saturated.
No closed revenue attributed. Networking ROI inverts at early stage — it optimizes for social proof you don't need yet.
SEO is a 6-12 month compounding asset. At 90-day $10K MRR target it is structurally misaligned. 2 posts in a month is effort without near-term payoff.
Fundraising narrative is downstream of traction. Deck updates before $10K MRR are premature optimization. Freeze until target is hit.
Brand-building channel with sub-3-month feedback loops. No evidence of conversion in current output data.
Useful quarterly. Running it continuously is anxiety management disguised as strategy.
Async Loom or Slack thread achieves the same coordination at zero synchronous time cost for a two-person team.

unconventional_reframes.conventional_assumption: More outreach channels = more pipeline coverage
unconventional_reframes.pareto_reframe: At 3 customers from inbound with zero from cold outreach, you already have a working acquisition channel. The Pareto move is to double inbound conversion speed, not add cold volume to a channel with no closes.

unconventional_reframes.conventional_assumption: Founders need to build product to grow
unconventional_reframes.pareto_reframe: You have 10 activities and 3 customers. None of those customers required new features to close — they closed on existing product. Until discovery calls surface a blocking feature, building is avoidance behavior.
True — but only after the business model is validated. At sub-$1K MRR, content is future-discounted work. Every blog post hour is an opportunity cost against 10 discovery calls.

action_matrix.action: Set inbound demo SLA: respond within 15 minutes during business hours. Build a Zapier/n8n trigger from form submission to calendar booking.
Schedule 3 customer discovery calls per day for the next 30 days. Script is secondary — volume is primary at this stage.
Pause cold email, networking, SEO, and competitive research entirely for 60 days. Redirect those hours to inbound follow-up and discovery.
Replace weekly standup with async Slack thread: 3 bullets — shipped, blocked, next. Reclaim 1hr/week.
After hitting $3K MRR, re-audit to find the Pareto layer inside discovery calls (which conversation type converts fastest).

recursive_note: At $10K MRR, re-run this audit. The trivial 80% will shift — cold outreach and content become viable once inbound is saturated. Pareto layers are stage-dependent.
